business.headline: Reliance Jio hires banks for IPO, no new capital to be raised: ReportThe IPO is expected to be structured as an "offer for sale" (OFS). This means existing investors will sell a part of their stake to the public, rather than the company issuing new shares.

Hidden reasons why life insurance claims get delayed or rejectedFiling a life insurance claim should be a straightforward process, especially during difficult times. Yet, many families face unexpected delays or rejections, often due to small details they overlooked. What really goes wrong?

Russian oil tanker bound for China diverts to India's New Mangalore PortA Russian oil tanker, Aqua Titan, originally bound for China, has diverted course in the South China Sea and is now headed to India's New Mangalore port, set to arrive on March 21st.

7 China-bound Russian oil tankers make U-turn, head to IndiaThe rerouting of these vessels carrying Russian oil comes after India received a temporary waiver from the US to buy 'sanctioned' Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

Iran-US war impact may push up medicine prices in IndiaPharma industry bodies have already raised the issue with the government, sending an emergency report highlighting the risk to raw material supplies.

Why borrowing now feels safer than saving for many IndiansToday, a growing number of Indians—especially younger, urban consumers—are embracing a very different model: spend first, borrow easily, and worry about repayment later.
Sensex ends 633 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 633.29 points to close at 76,704.13, while the NSE Nifty50 added 196.65 points to end at 23,777.80.

Fixed deposits are boring, but are they the smartest bet right now?Fixed deposits may have fallen out of favour in recent years, but they never really disappeared. Even as investors chased higher returns in equities and gold, a large share of Indian savings stayed parked in banks.

Delhi High Court dismisses Peru's plea for sole rights over 'PISCO' nameThe case goes back to 2005, when Peru applied for GI protection for 'PISCO' in India. Chile opposed the move, claiming it had long used the name for its own grape-based spirit.

Nithin Kamath criticises banking apps for seeking unnecessary permissionsKamath referred to the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP), which means apps should only ask for the minimum access required to function.

Why gold isn't rising despite Iran-US war and what it means for your moneyWar and uncertainty typically push gold prices higher, but the current trend tells a different story. Despite rising geopolitical tensions, gold is moving in a narrow range; why isn't it surging this time?

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is the stock market rising today?The Sensex was up 825.79 points, or 1.09%, at 76,896.63, while the Nifty 50 gained 239.10 points, or 1.01%, to trade at 23,820.25 as of 11:44 AM. It may be noted that the Sensex has gained over 2,000 points this week after a sharp losing spree.

Air India restores long-grounded Boeing 777, completes revival of 30 aircraftThe aircraft, a Boeing 777-300 ER registered as VT-ALL, returned to commercial operations in March 2026 after remaining out of service since February 2020.

IRDAI forms working groups to address gaps in health insurance ecosystemThe development comes amid ongoing concerns over claim delays, disputes between hospitals and insurers, and rising medical costs, all of which have affected policyholders' experience despite the growth of health insurance coverage.

Sensex up 2,000 points this week: Why is market up despite ongoing Iran war?The rebound on Dalal Street this week comes even as tensions in the Middle East continue, raising questions about what is driving the market higher despite global uncertainty.

Insurance regulator plans Public Insurance Registry. What it means for policyholdersThe registry is envisioned as a unified digital infrastructure covering the entire lifecycle of an insurance policy, from issuance and servicing to claims, grievance redressal and dispute resolution.
Urban Company share price jumps 15%. Why is it rising today?At around 10:06 am, the Urban Company was trading 13.25% higher at Rs 124.70. Earlier in the trading session, the stock was up around 15%.
Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why are IT stocks rising today?As of 9:49 am, major IT stocks such as Infosys, TCS and HCLTech were up around 1–3%, emerging among the key contributors to the Nifty's gains.

IDBI Bank share price jumps 4% after steep fall. What's driving the rebound?The stock had earlier fallen sharply after reports suggested that the government may revisit or restart the stake sale process due to lower-than-expected bids, raising concerns about delays in divestment.
Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 23,600; TCS up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex gained 456.82 points to 76,527.66, while the NSE Nifty50 added 144.25 points to 23,725.40 as of 9:30 am.

entertainment.headline: Men block cab, threaten Nimisha Nair in Mumbai; actor shares video Actor Nimisha Nair shared a disturbing harassment incident in Mumbai after two men allegedly blocked her cab and tried to intimidate her. The actor said that she experienced a similar incident earlier this year.

ZEE5, JioHotstar, SonyLIV this week: Chiraiya, Kasargod Embassy and more This week's regional OTT lineup brings a mix of powerful narratives, from rural resistance and social questioning to crime and historical drama.

Boney Kapoor loses 26 kg, says Sridevi would have loved to see him now Producer Boney Kapoor opened up about losing 26 kg and shares an emotional note about Sridevi. His latest post offered a glimpse into a deeply personal journey.

Dhurandhar The Revenge: Six burning questions we'll finally get answers to From Hamza's past to the truth behind key characters, Dhurandhar: The Revenge looks set to finally answer the many questions left behind by the first film. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film stars Ranveer Singh, Arjun Rampal, R Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun among others.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day trailer: Tom Holland faces mayhem in a darker new era The trailer for Tom Holland's Spider-Man: Brand New Day was released on March 18. The Phase 6 solo-hero-film from Marvel Studios depicts a darker new era.

KD - The Devil director defends Sarse Ninna song, urges to watch full video first The lyrical video of Sarse Ninna Seraga Sarse from KD: The Devil has sparked controversy online, with criticism centred on its lyrics, visuals and portrayal of women. Director Prem has responded, defending the song's use of metaphor and expressing confidence in the Censor Board.

Netflix, Prime Video this week: Peaky Blinders, Jatadhara, Border 2 and more This week's OTT lineup brings a diverse mix of entertainment, from war dramas and crime sagas to supernatural thrillers and global music events.

Knew we'd have issues: Nora Fatehi recalls her reaction on hearing KD song in Hindi Nora Fatehi said she was unaware of Sarke Chunar, the Hindi version of the controversial Sarse Ninna song from the Kannada film, KD - The Devil. She added that she doesn't understand Kannada, but the moment she heard the Hindi version she told the director the song would invite backlash. She has appealed to fans to refrain from sharing the track.

Dhurandhar 2: Fan theories around Ranveer Singh's film are getting louder As Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge approaches release, fan theories around unresolved plot points, hidden characters and a possible larger spy universe continue to build intrigue.

I stand by him till my dying breath: Celina Jaitly after brother refuses to talk Celina Jaitly recently shared a post on social media speaking about her brother Vikrant Jaitly's well-being amid his detention in the United Arab Emirates. Her post came after Vikrant refused to communicate with her following a petition she filed seeking assistance for him.

Khushbu Sundar, Vishnu Manchu lash out at AIADMK leader over Nayanthara remark Khushbu Sundar and Vishnu Manchu have strongly criticised AIADMK leader CV Shanmugam for his derogatory remarks against actress Nayanthara. The controversy sparked widespread backlash.

Main Aur Tu from Dhurandhar 2: Jasmine Sandlas' song brings bold beats and attitude After Aari Aari, Dhurandhar 2 makers released another track ahead of the grand release. Titled Main Aur Tu, the song is crooned by Jasmine Sandlas.

Ahead of Dhurandhar 2, controversies surrounding Ranveer Singh-Aditya Dhar film Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is set to release in theatres on March 19. Ahead of the grand release, take a look at the controversies surrounding the film.

10 years of Kapoor & Sons: How Sidharth Malhotra reacted after reading the script On the 10th anniversary of Kapoor & Sons, Sidharth Malhotra shared his emotional response to the script. The actor also reflected on his character and how the story still resonates with audiences.

Vir Das heads to Royal Albert Hall for first show with Hey Stranger tour Comedian Vir Das will bring his 'Hey Stranger' stand-up tour to London's Royal Albert Hall on April 1, joining a select group of Indian performers who have graced the historic venue.

BTS comeback concert in Seoul triggers terror alert amid frenzy BTS' comeback in Seoul draws 260,000 fans, triggers heightened security, and turns Gwanghwamun Square into a global spectacle streamed to nearly 190 countries.

Dhurandhar 2 paid premieres in Tamil and Telugu cancelled. Here's why The paid premiere shows of Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana have been cancelled due to unavailability of regional language content. Hindi version screenings will replace the regional shows.

Lollapalooza 2026 lineup: Charli XCX, Jennie and Lorde among headliners The Lollapalooza 2026 lineup features Charli XCX, JENNIE, and Lorde, marking a significant shift with women occupying 60% of the top-tier slots and showcasing a diverse, globalised range of musical talent.

No spoilers, please: Aditya Dhar urges fans as Dhurandhar 2 releases tomorrow Director Aditya Dhar urged fans to avoid sharing spoilers for Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He thanked viewers for the first film's success and called on them to keep the sequel's surprises intact ahead of its release on March 19.

general news.headline: CM barging in during ED raids not happy situation: Supreme Court blasts BengalThe Supreme Court's stinging remarks came while it was hearing the ED's petition alleging interference by Mamata Banerjee and her government during its January 8 raids on the office of political consultancy firm I-PAC.

7 China-bound Russian oil tankers make U-turn, head to IndiaThe rerouting of these vessels carrying Russian oil comes after India received a temporary waiver from the US to buy 'sanctioned' Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

US mercenary's India arrest rekindles Hasina's 'Christian state plot' warningTwo years ago, former Bangladesh PM Sheikh Hasina warned of an alleged plot by a 'white man' to establish a Christian state. In 2025, the Mizoram CM said Ukraine war veterans were using the state to enter Myanmar. The arrest of 7 foreign nationals by the NIA has prompted experts to suggest that the incidents might be connected.

How a loyal Congress MP from Assam was forced to join the BJPPradyut Bordoloi was a dyed-in-the-wool Congressi who vowed to never leave the party. The physical attack on him in 2025, and the Congress embracing "communal elements" pushed the Lok Sabha MP into joining the BJP. Bordoloi's exit from the Congress after three decades is significant for the party and Assam's politics.

She has appealed to fans to refrain from sharing the track.

Cost of living in India 2026: City-wise expenses and monthly costsCost of living in India 2026 varies by city, with Mumbai the most expensive and Kolkata more affordable. Check monthly expenses, rent, and city-wise cost comparison to plan your budget better.

Why India is best-placed to broker peace in US-Israel-Iran warThe President of Finland joined other global voices to suggest that India should mediate in the Iran-US-Israel war. Here's why India, a major economy with warm ties with Washington, Jerusalem, and Iran, and a heritage of non-alignment and strategic autonomy, might be the best bet to broker peace.

Petrol, gas crisis forces Pak to cancel Republic Day parade, celebrationsAmid a dire energy crisis triggered by the choking of the Strait of Hormuz, Islamabad has cancelled the celebrations and the parade scheduled for Pakistan Day. The March 23 observance will now be marked with "simplicity and dignity", said Pakistani PM Shahbaz Sharif's office, as fuel shortages and soaring costs hit the people hard.

Jobs: Nothing has changed in 40 years. 40% grads still unemployedNearly four in ten young graduates in India are struggling to find jobs despite rising access to higher education, highlighting a deep mismatch between degrees and employability. A new report by Azim Premji University reveals how this persistent trend is raising concerns about the country's ability to fully benefit from its demographic advantage.

Live: What if we finish off what's left in Iran, Trump's blunt threat amid warUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Israeli warplanes struck central Beirut early Wednesday, destroying apartment buildings in one of the most intense attacks on the Lebanese capital in decades. The strikes come as the Israel–Iran conflict widens across the region. Israel said it killed Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib a day after assassinating security chief Ali Larijani. Iran confirmed Larijani's death and retaliated by launching missiles with multiple warheads toward Israel, killing two people near Tel Aviv. Tehran said the loss of officials would not disrupt its operations, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi insisting the system does not rely on individuals. Meanwhile, Israel has increased strikes and ground operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, while Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, has rejected mediation efforts to reduce tensions. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Indians in Gulf forced to miss Eid with families as Middle East war disrupts travelAs Eid-ul-Fitr approaches, thousands of Indian workers in Gulf nations are unable to return home due to flight cancellations, exorbitant fares and job security concerns amid tensions involving the US, Israel and Iran. Families in India are bracing for a subdued festival marked by absence and anxiety.

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a negotiation-heavy process, with unclear rules and uneven power between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 aims to bring more structure, clarity, and balance to how homes are rented.

Dhurandhar 2 runtime sparks memes as fans apply for leave, pack suitcase, pillowsWith Dhurandhar: The Revenge set for a March 18 release, the internet is abuzz with jokes and memes, especially about the film's nearly four-hour runtime.

Dhurandhar 2 Movie Review And Release Live: Film's first paid preview show beginsDhurandhar 2 movie review and release live updates: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge hits theatres amid massive buzz, with early reactions, box office trends and audience response coming in.

SRH name Ishan Kishan as stand-in captain in absence of Pat CumminsIPL 2026: Wicketkeeper batter Ishan Kishan has been named the captain of SunRisers Hyderabad in the absence of Pat Cummins, who is set to miss the first few IPL games for the franchise. Kishan's promotion comes in the backdrop of his sensational outing in the T20 World Cup 2026.

This is America's war: Iran vows to keep fighting after Ali Larijani killingCommenting on the killing of Iran's top security official, Ali Larijani, the country's foreign minister said, "Even the (Supreme) Leader was martyred, yet the system continued its work."

Goodbye human coders? Sam Altman says thank you to developers as AI takes overSam Altman's heartfelt note to developers has reignited the debate around AI replacing coders. But current data and industry voices suggest humans still have a strong role to play. Here is everything you need to know.

Loved us, married Modi: Mallikarjun Kharge's Deve Gowda quip leaves PM in splitsA witty remark by Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge turned a serious parliamentary exchange into a moment of laughter, as his playful dig at former Prime Minister H D Deve Gowda left even the Prime Minister smiling.

During proceedings in Parliament, Kharge took a light-hearted swipe at Deve Gowda over his remarks praising Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

With a clever turn of phrase, Kharge said Gowda appeared to want a "partnership" with the Opposition but a "marriage" with the BJP. "Prem humare saath, shaadi Modi ji ke saath," he quipped, drawing laughter across the House.

Kharge's metaphor pointed to what the Opposition sees as mixed signals from Deve Gowda and his party in recent times.

While maintaining ties with Opposition parties, Gowda has also made remarks seen as appreciative of the BJP-led government, prompting political reactions.

Bengaluru family tries to courier a sack, employees shocked by what's insideSuspicious movements inside the sack prompted the staff to open it. What they found shocked everyone in the office – an elderly man struggling to breathe.

With Taj Mahal poster, US offers free flights, cash payouts for self-deporteesThe Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is using posters with images of India's Taj Mahal to promise free tickets and a cash payout of $2,600 to undocumented immigrants who voluntarily deport themselves from the US. Ironically, a monument for love has been turned into one of hate for immigrants.

The West is finally catching on to 2000s Bollywood fashionShort kurtas paired with loose pants and dupattas were a big trend in the 2000s Bollywood, and now, they seem to be catching on in the West too.

Is Bumrah set to lose Rs 2 crore despite T20 World Cup heroics?Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah was one of the standout performers in India's T20 World Cup campaign. Despite his exceptional outing, sections of the Indian media have reported that he could face a pay cut.
How animal cruelty affects your eggs and milk?If you think that animal welfare is not your concern, know that it shows in every ounce of milk you consume and your eggs.

Freedom of speech not absolute: Centre as 'Sarke Chunar' row reaches Lok SabhaThe controversy over the song "Sarke Chunar" reached the Lok Sabha, where the Centre defended its ban, saying freedom of speech is subject to "reasonable restrictions" in line with societal and cultural norms.

sports.headline: India drawn with China in Thomas and Uber Cups 2026 India have been handed tough draws in both the Thomas Cup and Uber Cup 2026 after being grouped with defending champions China. The BWF announced the draws for the competition, which is set to held  between April 24 and May 3.

FC Barcelona will be Hansi Flick's last coaching job Hansi Flick says Barcelona will be his last club as a coach, ahead of a crucial Champions League second leg against Newcastle at Camp Nou on Wednesday.

Salman Agha, Mehidy Hasan Miraz soar in ICC rankings after stellar ODI series Salman Agha and Mehidy Hasan Miraz headlined the latest ICC rankings update after Bangladesh's 2-1 ODI series win over Pakistan, with Salman rising to No.9 among batters and Mehidy climbing to No.7 among bowlers.

DC captain Axar given warning by Pujara ahead of IPL 2026: Don't be extra casual Former India cricketer Cheteshwar Pujara told Axar Patel not to get too casual when it comes to being the captain of Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Axar took over the reins last season for the Capitals.

Despite Ashes axe, Ollie Pope remains hopeful of England Test team return Ollie Pope remains hopeful of an England comeback after being dropped during the Ashes 2025-26, as he aims to regain form in county cricket. Pope admitted that him being dropped during the series against Australia was the right call in the end.

Sports ministry to review boycott stanceBangladesh sports minister Anamul Haque orders probe into the country's T20 World Cup boycott and BCB elections, plans talks with ICC over board's future and governance issues. Senegal lose AFCON title in bizarre decision overturn, Morocco crowned winnersMorocco has been awarded the 2026 African Cup of Nations title after the Confederation of ‌African Football (CAF) overturned the final win of Senegal. The CAF ruled that Senegal had forfeited their game after they left the field during the final following a controversial penalty being awarded to Morocco.

Emma Raducanu pulls out of Miami Open, continues struggling in challenging seasonBritish tennis star and 2021 US Open champion Emma Raducanu has withdrawn from the 2026 Miami Open due to illness and lingering post-viral symptoms, missing a chance to defend her 215 ranking points.

Did Kamran Akmal call Mohsin Naqvi a trophy chor after Bangladesh series loss?Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal slammed the team's poor performance in the series against Bangladesh, calling Pakistan cricket a comedy series. His comments also indirectly referenced PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, linked to the Asia Cup controversy.

Pratika Rawal recalls emotional ride in Women's World Cup final: Didn't see last ballPratika Rawal described the Women's World Cup final against South Africa as an emotional whirlwind, saying she didn't even watch the last ball. The right-handed batter missed the final due to an ankle and knee injury.

Mexico willing to host Iran's matches, after team refuses to play in USAMexico is willing to host Iran's FIFA World Cup 2026 matches after the West Asian nation expressed reluctance to play in the United States amid its ongoing conflict involving Israel. The Mexican President said the country would have no problem hosting the games.

No more impact sub, but Rohit might spend time on the bench, hints MI coachIPL: Senior Mumbai Indians batter Rohit Sharma will not be used as an impact substitute this season, head coach Mahela Jayawardene said during a media interaction on March 17.

Australia cricketers in doubt for PSL 2026? PCB opens up on security concerns reportThe PCB has dismissed concerns over overseas players' travel for Pakistan Super League 2026, insisting all arrangements are in place and Australian players who have signed up will begin arriving next week.

Pak-origin cricketer hits back at Gavaskar after Abrar Ahmed's signing in The HundredAzeem Rafiq hits out at Sunil Gavaskar over his remarks on Abrar Ahmed's Hundred signing, calling them "ridiculous" as the controversy around IPL-linked ownership and Pakistan players grows.

Conway sparks carnage as New Zealand crush South Africa to win 2nd T20I by 68 runsDevon Conway's 60 and a fiery pace display from Ben Sears and Lockie Ferguson helped New Zealand crush South Africa by 68 runs, levelling the T20I series after a dramatic collapse.

Sensational Amelia Kerr scripts history with 100 Women's T20I wicketsNZW vs SAW: New Zealand skipper Amelia Kerr became the fastest cricketer from a full-member nation to complete 1,000 runs and 100 wickets in Women's T20Is, reaching the milestone in her 94th match against South Africa.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
